title: Roofers
description: Cover roofs of structures with shingles, slate, asphalt, aluminum, wood, or related materials. May spray roofs, sidings, and walls with material to bind, seal, insulate, or soundproof sections of structures.

tasks.text: Inspect problem roofs to determine the best repair procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Remove snow, water, or debris from roofs prior to applying roofing materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up scaffolding to provide safe access to roofs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Estimate materials and labor required to complete roofing jobs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cement or nail flashing strips of metal or shingle over joints to make them watertight.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install partially overlapping layers of material over roof insulation surfaces, using chalk lines, gauges on shingling hatchets, or lines on shingles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut felt, shingles, or strips of flashing to fit angles formed by walls, vents, or intersecting roof surfaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply plastic coatings, membranes, fiberglass, or felt over sloped roofs before applying shingles.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install, repair, or replace single-ply roofing systems, using waterproof sheet materials such as modified plastics, elastomeric, or other asphaltic compositions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach roofing paper to roofs in overlapping strips to form bases for other materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cover roofs or exterior walls of structures with slate, asphalt, aluminum, wood, gravel, gypsum, or related materials, using brushes, knives, punches, hammers, or other tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Waterproof or damp-proof walls, floors, roofs, foundations, or basements by painting or spraying surfaces with waterproof coatings or by attaching waterproofing membranes to surfaces.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply reflective roof coatings, such as special paints or single-ply roofing sheets, to existing roofs to reduce solar heat absorption.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Apply alternate layers of hot asphalt or tar and roofing paper to roofs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install vapor barriers or layers of insulation on flat roofs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cover exposed nailheads with roofing cement or caulking to prevent water leakage or rust.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Smooth rough spots to prepare surfaces for waterproofing, using hammers, chisels, or rubbing bricks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Glaze top layers to make a smooth finish or embed gravel in the bitumen for rough surfaces.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mop or pour hot asphalt or tar onto roof bases.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install attic ventilation systems, such as turbine vents, gable or ridge vents, or conventional or solar-powered exhaust fans.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Install skylights on roofs to increase natural light inside structures or to reduce energy costs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply gravel or pebbles over top layers of roofs, using rakes or stiff-bristled brooms.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Spray roofs, sidings, or walls to bind, seal, insulate, or soundproof sections of structures, using spray guns, air compressors, or heaters.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach solar panels to existing roofs, according to specifications and without damaging roofing materials or the structural integrity of buildings.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Punch holes in slate, tile, terra cotta, or wooden shingles, using punches and hammers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Apply modular soil- and plant-containing grids over existing roof membranes to create green roofs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install layers of vegetation-based green roofs, including protective membranes, drainage, aeration, water retention and filter layers, soil substrates, irrigation materials, and plants.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
